name: AdV5_E1A_E1B_oncogene_region
description: AdV5 early region E1A/E1B at the left end of the genome (just downstream of left ITR), encoding the immediate-early transactivators E1A (binds pRB, p400, p300/CBP) and E1B (E1B-55K binds p53; E1B-19K is a Bcl-2 homolog). In Ad5-based gene-therapy and oncolytic-virus vectors (e.g. ONYX-015, dl1520), E1B-55K is deleted to restrict replication to p53-null tumor cells. Highly conserved across species C; species F (Ad40/41) E1A has a divergent CR3 region that contributes to enteric tropism. Not transforming for human cells in vivo but transforms rodent cells in culture (the basis of the classical 'oncogenic-adenovirus' literature, which does not extend to natural human disease).
